response.justification: Strong technical generalist with deep open-source roots, Linux/Unix exposure, architecture and self-driven leadership (founder, mentoring, organizing dojos), which aligns well with the open-source and digital-sovereignty focus of the role. However, German is listed as only Basic while the role explicitly requires C1 minimum, a hard gate for client-facing leadership in this market, and there is little evidence of formal team management or technical project management at the scale this executive role demands. Python is also not explicitly demonstrated.
strengths: Deep open-source and Linux/Unix background plus self-built libraries and a GIS platform
Broad full-stack architecture experience with TDD/CI/CD craftsmanship and a CS degree
Demonstrated mentoring, teaching, and founder-level initiative
concerns: German only Basic vs. required C1 minimum — likely disqualifying
No explicit formal team-management or technical project-management leadership at executive scale
Python not evidenced despite being a desired skill
